author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

title: Chapter XIX. 31–42, and XX. 1-9.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After that the Lord Jesus had accomplished all that He foreknew required accomplishment before His death, and had, when it pleased Himself, given up the ghost, what followed thereafter, as related by the evangelist, let us now consider.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Jews therefore,” he says, “because it was the preparation (parasceve), that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the Sabbath-day (for that Sabbath-day was an high day), besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that their legs might be taken away, but the persons themselves whose legs were broken for the purpose of effecting their death, and permitting them to be detached from the tree, lest their continuing to hang on the crosses should defile the great festal day by the horrible spectacle of their day-long torments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then came the soldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other who was crucified with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when they came to Jesus, and saw that He was dead already, they brake not His legs: but one of the soldiers with a spear laid open His side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A suggestive word was made use of by the evangelist, in not saying pierced, or wounded His side, or anything else, but “opened;” that thereby, in a sense, the gate of life might be thrown open, from whence have flowed forth the sacraments of the Church, without which there is no entrance to the life which is the true life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That blood was shed for the remission of sins; that water it is that makes up the health-giving cup, and supplies at once the laver of baptism and water for drinking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was announced beforehand, when Noah was commanded to make a door in the side of the ark, whereby the animals might enter which were not destined to perish in the flood, and by which the Church was prefigured.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because of this, the first woman was formed from the side of the man when asleep, and was called Life, and the mother of all living.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Truly it pointed to a great good, prior to the great evil of the transgression (in the guise of one thus lying asleep).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This second Adam bowed His head and fell asleep on the cross, that a spouse might be formed for Him from that which flowed from the sleeper’s side.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O death, whereby the dead are raised anew to life!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can be purer than such blood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more health-giving than such a wound?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he that saw it,” he says, “bare record, and his record is true; and he knoweth that he saith true, that ye also might believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not, That ye also might know, but “that ye might believe;” for he knoweth who hath seen, that he who hath not seen might believe his testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And believing belongs more to the nature of faith than seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what else is meant by believing than giving to faith a suitable reception?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For these things were done,” he adds, “that the scripture should be fulfilled, A bone of Him ye shall not break.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And again, another scripture saith, They shall look on Him whom they pierced.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has furnished two testimonies from the Scriptures for each of the things which he has recorded as having been done.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to the words, “But when they came to Jesus, and saw that He was dead already, they brake not His legs,” belongeth the testimony, “A bone of Him ye shall not break:” an injunction which was laid upon those who were commanded to celebrate the passover by the sacrifice of a sheep in the old law, which went before as a shadow of the passion of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence “our passover has been offered, even Christ,” of whom the prophet Isaiah also had predicted, “He shall be led as a lamb to the slaughter.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner to the words which he subjoined, “But one of the soldiers laid open His side with a spear,” belongeth the other testimony, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced;” where Christ is promised in the very flesh wherein He was afterwards to come to be crucified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And after this, Joseph of Arimathea (being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews) besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus: and Pilate gave him leave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came therefore, and took the body of Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there came also Nicodemus, who came to Jesus by night at first, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are not to explain the meaning by saying, “first bringing a mixture of myrrh,” but by attaching the word “first” to the preceding clause.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Nicodemus had at first come to Jesus by night, as recorded by this same John in the earlier portions of his Gospel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the statement given us here, therefore, we are to understand that Nicodemus came to Jesus, not then only, but then for the first time; and that he was a regular comer afterwards, in order by hearing to become a disciple; which is certified, nowadays at least, to almost all nations in the revelation of the body of the most blessed Stephen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then took they the body of Jesus, and wound it in linen clothes with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The evangelist, I think, was not without a purpose in so framing his words, “as the manner of the Jews is to bury;” for in this way, unless I am mistaken, he has admonished us that, in duties of this kind, which are observed to the dead, the customs of every nation ought to be preserved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now in the place where He was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As in the womb of the Virgin Mary no one was conceived before Him, and no one after Him, so in this sepulchre there was no one buried before Him, and no one after Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “There laid they Jesus therefore, because of the Jews’ preparation; for the sepulchre was nigh at hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would have us to understand that the burial was hurried, lest the evening should overtake them; when it was no longer permitted to do any such thing, because of the preparation, which the Jews among us are more in the habit of calling in Latin, cœna pura (the pure meal).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And on the first of the week came Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and saw the stone taken away from the sepulchre.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The first of the week is what Christian practice now calls the Lord’s day, because of the resurrection of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “She ran, therefore, and came to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some of the Greek codices have, “They have taken my Lord,” which may likely enough have been said by the stronger than ordinary affection of love and handmaid relationship; but we have not found it in the several codices to which we have had access.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So they ran both together: and that other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The repetition here is worthy of notice and of commendation for the way in which a return is made to what had previously been omitted, and yet is added just as if it followed in due order.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after having already said, “they came to the sepulchre,” he goes back to tell us how they came, and says, “so they ran both together,” etc. Where he shows that, by outrunning his companion, there came first to the sepulchre that other disciple, by whom he means himself, while he relates all as if speaking of another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he stooping down,” he says, “saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and saw the linen clothes lying, and the napkin, which had been about His head, not lying with the linen clothes, but folded up in one place by itself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do we suppose these things have no meaning?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I can suppose no such thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we hasten on to other points, on which we are compelled to linger by the need there is for investigation, or some other kind of obscurity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in such things as are self-manifest, the inquiry into the meaning even of individual details is, indeed, a subject of holy delight, but only for those who have leisure, which is not the case with us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then went in also that other disciple who had come first to the sepulchre.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came first, and entered last.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This also of a certainty is not without a meaning, but I am without the leisure needful for its explanation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he saw, and believed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here some, by not giving due attention, suppose that John believed that Jesus had risen again; but there is no indication of this from the words that follow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what does he mean by immediately adding, “For as yet they knew not the scripture, that He must rise again from the dead”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He could not then have believed that He had risen again, when he did not know that it behoved Him to rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then did he see? what was it that he believed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What but this, that he saw the sepulchre empty, and believed what the woman had said, that He had been taken away from the tomb?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For as yet they knew not the scripture, that He must rise again from the dead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus also when they heard of it from the Lord Himself, although it was uttered in the plainest terms, yet from their custom of hearing Him speaking by parables, they did not understand, and believed that something else was His meaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we shall put off what follows till another discourse.
